subject: Human Resources
title: HR Budgeting
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: HR Budgeting.

hook.text: An HR department requests funding for a wellness program. Finance says: 'Prove it's worth the money.'

buildup.text: HR budgeting allocates funds across recruiting, training, compensation, benefits, technology, and compliance.

discovery.text: The key is tying every dollar to a business outcome—reduced turnover, faster hiring, or fewer incidents.

twist.text: HR departments that can't quantify their ROI are the first to face budget cuts during downturns.

climax.text: Strategic budgeting turns HR from a cost center into a measurable driver of business performance.

punchline.text: Every HR dollar should tell a business story.

quiz.question: How can HR justify its budget?
options: By tying spending to measurable business outcomes
By spending as little as possible
